pairs.question: When and where was the 2017 World Snooker Championship held?
pairs.answer: The 2017 World Snooker Championship took place from 15 April to 1 May 2017 at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, England.

pairs.question: How many ranking events were there in the 2016–17 snooker season before the World Championship?
pairs.answer: The 2017 World Snooker Championship was the 19th ranking event of the 2016–17 season, held after the China Open.

pairs.question: Who won the 2017 World Snooker Championship, and how many times had they won the title previously?
pairs.answer: Mark Selby won the title, securing his third World Championship victory after previously winning in 2014 and 2016.

pairs.question: What was the score in the final of the 2017 World Snooker Championship, and how did the winner recover from a deficit?
pairs.answer: Mark Selby defeated John Higgins 18–15 in the final. Selby overcame a 4–10 deficit in the second session to win the match.

pairs.question: What was the total prize fund for the 2017 World Snooker Championship, and how much did the winner receive?
pairs.answer: The total prize fund was £1,750,000, with the winner, Mark Selby, receiving £375,000 as the top prize.

pairs.question: How many century breaks occurred during the main stage of the 2017 World Snooker Championship, and who made the highest break?
pairs.answer: There were 74 century breaks in the main stage of the championship. Ronnie O'Sullivan made the highest break of 146 in the quarter-finals.

pairs.question: Which player scored a maximum break of 147 in the qualifying rounds of the 2017 World Snooker Championship?
pairs.answer: Gary Wilson scored a maximum break of 147 in qualifying during his first-round win over Josh Boileau.

pairs.question: Which broadcasters covered the 2017 World Snooker Championship in Europe and internationally?
pairs.answer: The tournament was broadcast in Europe by the BBC and Eurosport, and internationally by World Snooker on Facebook.
